title: Orchestrated build workflow — one channel, six runtimes

segments.subtitle: agent-garrison · the orchestrator routes each step to a fit-for-purpose runtime: Sonnet · Ollama · Opus · GPT (Codex) · Gemini · Opus · 2026-06-17

beats.caption: The orchestrator's policy (profile: build) — each task-type role maps to a fit-for-purpose runtime/model
beats.expectedScreen: the Model Router 'Role → target (build)' table: standard→cc-sonnet-med, fast→sdk-ollama-chat, expert→cc-opus-high, review→sec-codex, image→sec-gemini, video→cc-opus-high, with an 'ollama-local' badge

beats.caption: Step 1 — build the data model → Claude Sonnet (a bounded build task)
beats.expectedScreen: the chat with the data-model reply (fields/types for a token-bucket rate limiter) and the status strip showing model: sonnet

actions.text: Build step 1 — define the data model: the fields and types for a token-bucket rate limiter. About 5 lines.

beats.caption: Step 2 — a trivial helper → local Ollama via the Agent SDK (free, fenced, non-Anthropic)
beats.expectedScreen: the chat with a one-line helper reply and the status strip showing runtime: agent-sdk · provider: ollama-local · model: qwen3:0.6b

actions.text: Build step 2 — write a small, simple helper function that returns the current time in milliseconds. One line.

beats.caption: Step 3 — the complex core algorithm → Claude Opus (the hard build task)
beats.expectedScreen: the chat with the core token-bucket algorithm code and the status strip showing model: opus

actions.text: Build step 3 — implement the complex core token-bucket refill-and-consume algorithm. About 8 lines.

beats.caption: Step 4 — code review → GPT via the Codex runtime
beats.expectedScreen: the chat with a code-review reply listing correctness issues, and the status strip showing runtime: codex · provider: openai · model: gpt-5-codex

actions.text: Review step — review this token-bucket rate limiter for correctness bugs: it refills tokens as elapsed*rate without capping at capacity, and reads the clock twice per request. List 2 concrete issues, briefly.

beats.caption: Step 5 — apply the review fixes → Gemini
beats.expectedScreen: the chat with corrected code for the two issues, and the status strip showing runtime: gemini · provider: google · model: gemini-2.5-flash

actions.text: Implement the code-review fixes for the rate limiter. The review found: (1) token refill is not capped at capacity; (2) the clock is read twice per request. Reply with ONLY the corrected one-line code for each fix. Do not use any tools and do not read or edit files.

beats.caption: Step 6 — write the tests → Claude Opus (back to the orchestrator)
beats.expectedScreen: the chat with a short test suite (about 6 cases) for the rate limiter, and the status strip showing model: opus

actions.text: Write tests for the token-bucket rate limiter: list about 6 test cases as a short test suite.

segments.caption: The orchestrator's decision log — six steps, six runtime/model choices
segments.expectedScreen: terminal listing the 6 steps: build data model → cc-sonnet-med (claude-code/sonnet); helper → sdk-ollama-chat (agent-sdk/qwen3:0.6b); core algo → cc-opus-high (opus); review → sec-codex (codex/gpt-5-codex); apply fixes → sec-gemini (gemini); test → cc-opus-high (opus)

segments.text: One channel · one orchestrator · six runtimes
segments.subtitle: build → Sonnet / Ollama / Opus · review → GPT (Codex) · fixes → Gemini · test → Opus · runtime+model logged per step
